summary: The European Union coordinates the post-quantum transition through a Commission Recommendation and a Coordinated Implementation Roadmap, asking Member States to migrate in a synchronised way, with ENISA providing technical studies and ETSI developing European quantum-safe standards. A 2026 legislative proposal would require Member States to adopt post-quantum migration policies.
govActors:
European Commission | Issues the Coordinated Implementation Roadmap and Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 and the proposal to require Member State PQC migration policies
ENISA | EU Agency for Cybersecurity, publishes studies on the state of post-quantum standardisation and integration
ETSI | European standards body developing quantum-safe standards including a hybrid key exchange standard and the TC ESI PQC working group
NIS Cooperation Group | Sets out the Coordinated Implementation Roadmap recommendations for Member States
European Parliament | Resolution on the EU Cybersecurity Strategy calling for further research into post-quantum algorithms
EPRS | European Parliamentary Research Service, briefings on cryptographic security and digital sovereignty
EESC | Opinion noting the need to transition to quantum-resistant cryptography
ECB | Speeches and the Pontes user requirements document requiring post-quantum resilient cryptographic capabilities through crypto-agility
provenance: summary + govActors (Commission, NIS Cooperation Group): Coordinated Implementation Roadmap and Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 results rows
mandatory migration policy: COM(2026) 13 final results row
ENISA role: ENISA current-state, integration-study and news results rows

ETSI role + dominantProcess Mixed: ETSI hybrid key exchange standard and TC ESI PQC working group rows, set alongside ENISA work and references to the NIST process (ECB speech notes NIST finalised the first standards). Camp is genuinely blended (European ENISA/ETSI work plus NIST references), so Mixed assigned conservatively - REVIEW
ECB Pontes crypto-agility: ECB Pontes user requirements row
dominantProcess: assigned Mixed rather than omitted because both European (ETSI/ENISA) and NIST references are attested in included docs - REVIEW

targetCompletion / migrationTimeline (the EU 2030 date): OMITTED. A 2030 critical-infrastructure target appears only in third-party write-ups (industrialcyber, LinkedIn, medium) in candidates, not in the included EUR-Lex/europa.eu institutional summaries. Not attested by an institutional document, so left out per the iron rule - REVIEW
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED, no specific FIPS or algorithm names stated in the included EU document summaries
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / contextualiser, Medium): Supranational coordinator/conditioner: produces regulation and the coordinated roadmap, no algorithm of its own.

mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on PQC | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2025) | EU | soft-law
obligation: No binding PQC-specific requirement at EU level; member states are encouraged to publish national roadmaps and migrate on the coordinated 2026/2030/2035 timeline, while the NIS2 'state-of-the-art' duty is expected to absorb post-quantum requirements over time.
